title: [LLVM][PhaseOrdering] Run CSE after InstCombine has cleaned the result of vectorisation.
summary:
The developers generalized an optimization pattern by moving an EarlyCSE pass earlier in the pipeline, right after the main InstCombine pass following loop vectorization. This captures CSE opportunities created by InstCombine's operand replacements, which frequently arise from boolean operations generated during if-conversion. The key insight is that many vectorized loops produce similar intermediate expressions that InstCombine can canonicalize, making them identical and ripe for common subexpression elimination.

The patch restructures the pass order: EarlyCSE now runs unconditionally after InstCombine, before any further loop or control-flow optimizations. This ensures CSE is applied early when patterns are most uniform, reducing redundant computations before subsequent transforms. The conditional "ExtraVectorizerPasses" logic is nested within this new EarlyCSE pass, maintaining specialized cleanups for higher optimization levels.

Generalization strategy: Identify a recurring optimization opportunity (CSE after canonicalization), decouple it from specific loop transformations, and apply it earlier and more broadly in the pipeline to benefit multiple patterns beyond the original test case.
